growth_records.strain: digester sludge-derived enrichment, transferred >=6x on MeOH
growth_records.medium: Bicarbonate-buffered mineral salt medium (methylotrophic mixed culture)

up.units: umol MeOH supplied per bottle (0.9 mmol), batch

sec.method: whole-cell suspension assay, GC-FID, Michaelis-Menten fit

sec.units: umol produced per bottle from 0.9 mmol MeOH (batch)

growth_records.cite: Yin, Yongchao et al. Nitrous oxide inhibition of methanogenesis represents an underappreciated greenhouse gas emission feedback. The ISME Journal (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrae027. [PMC10960958]
growth_records.snippet: the mixed cultures produced 542.6 +/- 23.3 ... umol of CH4 from 0.9 mmol of MeOH ... Vmax ... 209.0 +/- 4.4 nmol CH4 min-1 mg protein-1 ... Km 359.3 +/- 30.8 uM ... KI 109.9 +/- 6.8 uM
growth_records.notes: Mixed methanogenic enrichment on MeOH (methylotrophic, Eq.1). Measured Vmax(CH4)=209.0 nmol/min/mg protein at 0 uM N2O. Km(MeOH)=359.3 uM. KI(N2O)=109.9+/-6.8 uM. Derived MeOH uptake at Vmax = 209.0 x 4/3 = 278.7 nmol/min/mg. Bulk: 0.9 mmol MeOH -> 542.6 umol CH4. Growth yield 0.6e7 cells/umol CH4 = 4.30 ug biomass/umol CH4 (2.5 16S copies/cell assumed). Dominant taxa: Methanomethylovorans, Methanomassiliicoccus. N2O reduced Vmax by 37.4% (50uM) and 75.4% (100uM); 30 uM N2O abolished growth.

growth_records.strain: digester sludge-derived enrichment, transferred >=6x on H2/CO2
growth_records.medium: Bicarbonate-buffered mineral salt medium (hydrogenotrophic mixed culture)

up.units: umol H2 supplied per bottle (1.24 mmol), batch

sec.method: whole-cell suspension assay, GC-FID, Michaelis-Menten fit

sec.units: umol produced per bottle from 1.24 mmol H2 (batch)

growth_records.cite: Yin, Yongchao et al. Nitrous oxide inhibition of methanogenesis represents an underappreciated greenhouse gas emission feedback. The ISME Journal (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrae027. [PMC10960958]
growth_records.snippet: the mixed cultures produced ... 316.5 +/- 25.4 ... umol of CH4 from ... 1.24 mmol of H2 ... Vmax ... 105.8 +/- 3.7 nmol CH4 min-1 mg protein-1 ... Km 19.0 +/- 2.3 uM ... KI 62.1 +/- 6.4 uM
growth_records.notes: Mixed enrichment on H2/CO2 (hydrogenotrophic, Eq.2). Measured Vmax(CH4)=105.8 nmol/min/mg protein at 0 uM N2O. Km(H2)=19.0 uM. KI(N2O)=62.1+/-6.4 uM. Derived H2 uptake at Vmax = 105.8 x 4 = 423.2 nmol/min/mg. Bulk: 1.24 mmol H2 -> 316.5 umol CH4. Growth yield 0.6e5 cells/umol CH4 = 0.43 ug biomass/umol CH4. Dominant taxa: Methanobacterium/Methanobacteriaceae. N2O reduced Vmax by 32.3% (30uM), 65.4% (60uM); 30 uM N2O abolished growth; up to 20% of added N2O consumed in H2/MeOH mixed vessels.

growth_records.strain: digester sludge-derived enrichment, transferred >=6x on acetate
growth_records.medium: Bicarbonate-buffered mineral salt medium (acetoclastic mixed culture)

up.units: umol acetate supplied per bottle (0.6 mmol), batch

sec.method: whole-cell suspension assay, GC-FID, Michaelis-Menten fit

sec.units: umol produced per bottle from 0.6 mmol acetate (batch)

growth_records.cite: Yin, Yongchao et al. Nitrous oxide inhibition of methanogenesis represents an underappreciated greenhouse gas emission feedback. The ISME Journal (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrae027. [PMC10960958]
growth_records.snippet: the mixed cultures produced ... 589.2 +/- 33.7 umol of CH4 from ... 0.6 mmol of acetate ... Vmax ... 22.8 +/- 0.7 nmol CH4 min-1 mg protein-1 ... Km 302.2 +/- 46.2 uM ... KI 17.7 +/- 1.8 uM
growth_records.notes: Mixed enrichment on acetate (acetoclastic, Eq.3). Measured Vmax(CH4)=22.8 nmol/min/mg protein at 0 uM N2O. Km(acetate)=302.2 uM. KI(N2O)=17.7+/-1.8 uM (lowest KI = most N2O-sensitive of all conditions). Acetate uptake = CH4 Vmax (1:1) = 22.8 nmol/min/mg. Bulk: 0.6 mmol acetate -> 589.2 umol CH4. Growth yield 1.0e6 cells/umol CH4 = 0.65 ug biomass/umol CH4. Dominant taxon: Methanosarcina. N2O reduced Vmax by 38% (10uM), 74.9% (30uM); 30 uM N2O abolished growth.
